1-1.description: Accomplish incomprehensible plans that lead it to act in seemingly random ways.

2-2.description: Learn more of the world by kidnapping people and consuming their minds.

3-3.description: Manipulate innocents into worshiping it as a god by using its telepathy from hiding.

4-4.description: Open a gate to the distant past or future, releasing an invasion from another time.

5-5.description: Rouse a dragon turtle, a kraken, or another sea monster to flood a coastal city.

6-6.description: Trick treasure hunters into recovering relics from its long-fallen empire.

1-1.description: Cumulus or cirrus clouds.

2-2.description: A mixture of vibrantly colored gases.

3-3.description: A pungent, sour-looking miasma

4-4.description: Shifting cloud clusters that resemble animals and simple shapes.

5-5.description: Sinister features obscured in a misty mass.

6-6.description: Swirling storm clouds.

1-1.description: A Celestial or Fiend using the object to protect or torment a mortal.

2-2.description: A combination of magic and technology, such as alchemy or alien science.

3-3.description: The essence of someone transformed by a supernatural trickster.

4-4.description: Fey as part of their games or wiles.

5-5.description: A magic-user in need of a guardian or servant.

6-6.description: Happenstance, with the item gaining a semblance of life after a hundred years of use.

7-7.description: The song of a magical instrument.

8-8.description: A spirit possessing the object.

9-9.description: Wild magic, a spell that went awry, or a chaotic Artifact.

10-10.description: The will of a powerful psychic being.

1-1.description: Another tunnel (either natural or of worked stone) that extends into the Underdark.

2-2.description: A buried ruin or grave exposed by the tunnel.

3-3.description: A cluster of 1d4 fresh ankheg eggs that can be broken and used as vials of Acid.

4-4.description: A dead ankheg and evidence of a deadlier subterranean predator.

5-5.description: A piece of ankheg carapace usable as a Shield.

6-6.description: A pouch with 2d6 GP near a puddle of acid.

7-7.description: A stray farm or woodland animal.

8-8.description: A viciously mauled scarecrow.

1-1.description: The bones of the arch-hag’s first love.

2-2.description: A devil’s tear.

3-3.description: An egg with a miniature castle inside.

4-4.description: A flower that blooms only when time stops.

5-5.description: A gift from the hag’s twin.

6-6.description: The multiverse’s worst pun.

7-7.description: One of the hag’s missing teeth.

8-8.description: Snow from the top of Mount Celestia.

9-9.description: A star pulled from the sky.

10-10.description: A thread from the Lady of Pain’s robes.

1-1.description: Arranging their victims in artful tableaux.

2-2.description: Hiding within large objects, such as suits of armor or hollow furnishings.

3-3.description: Leaving behind a signature item, such as a calling card, flower, seashell, or tooth.

4-4.description: Posing as celebrities, holy people, or servants.

5-5.description: Taking trophies from their victims.

6-6.description: Using poison with a distinctive color or smell.

1-1.description: Fights only oppressors.

2-2.description: Is an ex-soldier who was discarded by their nation and now takes what they were promised.

3-3.description: Is in a gang that views nonmembers as foes.

4-4.description: Hesitantly serves a villainous leader.

5-5.description: Secretly works for a government or a regional ruler to sow chaos.

6-6.description: Takes what they need to survive.

1-1.description: Appear prior to a family member’s death.

2-2.description: Haunt the site where it was executed.

3-3.description: Lament a lost love and haunt their grave.

4-4.description: Presage a disaster or tragedy.

5-5.description: Seek the return of a stolen treasure.

6-6.description: Slay those more beautiful than it was in life.

1-1.description: An ancient black dragon’s acidic saliva.

2-2.description: The blood or extreme emotions of a foul deity.

3-3.description: Cosmic entropy or ruinous planar forces.

4-4.description: A curse that transformed a forgotten tyrant.

5-5.description: Forbidden or industrialized magic.

6-6.description: Necrotic material animated by aimless spirits.

1-1.description: An Amulet of the Planes.

2-2.description: An Artifact of the DM’s choice.

3-3.description: The corpses of two gods who were entangled in battle when the blob consumed them.

4-4.description: A Cubic Gate.

5-5.description: A Deck of Many Things.

6-6.description: A magic key that opens a door in Sigil that no other key and no spell can open.

7-7.description: The preserved corpse of an empyrean.

8-8.description: The remains of half a kraken.

9-9.description: The skull of a death god.

1-1.description: Capture a soul that escaped the Nine Hells.

2-2.description: Convey a message or make an example of someone in the name of an archdevil.

3-3.description: Find someone who broke a deal with a devil.

4-4.description: Slay someone or steal something as part of its pact with a wicked magic-user.

1-1.description: Being possessed by a fiendish being.

2-2.description: Being resurrected by an evil magic-user.

3-3.description: Lengthy exposure to a Lower Plane.

4-4.description: Making a bargain with a Fiend.

5-5.description: Suffering a god’s curse.

6-6.description: Taking part in fiendish rituals.

Chain Devil Masks.name: Chain Devil Masks
Chain Devil Masks.description: To a Viewer, the Chain Devil Looks Like …

1-1.description: The corpse of a loved one.

2-2.description: A disapproving deity.

3-3.description: A harsh instructor or superior.

4-4.description: The viewer at their lowest point in life.

1-1.description: Block the path of anyone who enters a site with a weapon drawn.

2-2.description: Defend any member of their creator’s family or community who is threatened in its sight.

3-3.description: Prevent any Fiend from crossing a bridge.

4-4.description: Remove any who enter its creator’s workshop.

1-1.description: The symbol is an alchemical sign

2-2.description: The symbol is an animal

3-3.description: The symbol is a celestial body

4-4.description: The symbol is a deity’s icon

5-5.description: The symbol is an element

6-6.description: The symbol is an eye

7-7.description: The symbol is a geometric shape

8-8.description: The symbol is a letter or number

9-9.description: The symbol is part of a monster

1-1.description: Depicted as A calendar or map

2-2.description: Depicted as A crest or as heraldry

3-3.description: Depicted as An elaborate diagram

4-4.description: Depicted as A metaphorical image

5-5.description: Depicted as A mystical being

6-6.description: Depicted as Part of an equation

7-7.description: Depicted as A repeating pattern

8-8.description: Depicted as A series of scratches

1-1.description: Bring about the end of a dominant order, an age, or the world.

2-2.description: Expand their faith though mind control or supernatural coercion.

3-3.description: Burn away the comfortable lies of reality, revealing forgotten or terrible truths.

4-4.description: Make global changes, like sinking the land or awakening volcanoes.

5-5.description: Remake life on a mass scale, altering other creatures’ bodies or spiritual beings.

6-6.description: Summon their deity or its herald, weapon, or realm into their world.

1-1.description: Marks from canine jaws to appear on the victim’s body, as if they were still being mauled.

2-2.description: The victim’s body to wither, as if constantly exposed to desert heat.

3-3.description: The victim to be distracted by distant howling or vague whispers only they can hear.

4-4.description: The victim’s flesh to rot like a corpse.

5-5.description: The victim to itch, as if they had fleas or sand beneath their skin.

6-6.description: Wicked symbols to gradually appear on and spread across the victim’s body.

1-1.description: Break open a vault and steal what’s within.

2-2.description: Splugawić miejsce bluźnierczymi symbolami i demoniczną krwią.

3-3.description: Fetch or otherwise provide materials for a profane ritual.

4-4.description: Guard a site and slay anyone who comes near.

5-5.description: Hunt down a foe, destroying everything barring the demon’s path.

6-6.description: Intimidate someone into following orders.

Deva Messages.name: Deva Messages
Deva Messages.description: The Deva Tasks a Mortal with Delivering …

1-1.description: The corpse of a hero in need of redemption.

2-2.description: The cure for a plague in a distant land.

3-3.description: A holy coffer that must not be opened.

4-4.description: A magic weapon usable only by a true hero.

5-5.description: A seedling that wilts if exposed to anger.

6-6.description: Someone from another world with a prophesied purpose but no memory.

1-1.description: Cause chaos within the temple of a deity that cursed it to live without a true form.

2-2.description: Conceal evidence of a vast conspiracy.

3-3.description: Control a community through fear by posing as a legendary bogeyman.

4-4.description: Replace a noble to enjoy a decadent lifestyle.

5-5.description: Spy on wizards to learn how to complete its own botched magical creation.

6-6.description: Take an influential position, acting as a sleeper agent for a doppelganger invasion.

Dracolich Soul Gem Vessels.name: Dracolich Soul Gem Vessels
Dracolich Soul Gem Vessels.description: A Dracolich’s Soul Gem Is Hidden In …

1-1.description: Another dragon’s treasure hoard.

2-2.description: The body of a servant or an ancestor.

3-3.description: The core of a dracolich’s melted hoard.

4-4.description: A corrupted dragon egg.

5-5.description: A dragon horn a hero took as a trophy.

6-6.description: A nation’s royal or religious treasure.

7-7.description: A powerful magic item.

8-8.description: A source of magical wonders, such as a giant tree or mystical pool.

9-9.description: The vault of an archdevil, a wicked god, or another extraplanar villain.

10-10.description: The weapon that slew the dracolich.

1-1.description: A blessing from a deity of assassins, dangerous wildernesses, or the Underdark.

2-2.description: A curse from a powerful hag, vengeful witch, or strange Artifact.

3-3.description: An experiment by an aboleth, a mind flayer, or another life-shaping magic-user.

4-4.description: A magical means of escaping disaster or some worse fate.

5-5.description: A mutation after exposure to chaotic planar energies or strange Underdark radiations.

6-6.description: A punishment from a spiteful god, like Lolth or the Queen of Air and Darkness.

1-1.description: An avenger who strikes against destructive civilizations and those who abuse nature.

2-2.description: A guide who aids travelers in navigating the realms of Beasts, Fey, or Plants.

3-3.description: A hermit who works alone to protect the lands, seas, or skies they call home.

4-4.description: A mender who travels the world healing natural, magical, or manufactured disasters.

5-5.description: Part of a loose organization that adheres to timeless rituals and guards natural secrets.

6-6.description: A warden who minds the underpinnings of reality and protects against extraplanar threats.

1-1.description: An acres-large clonal colony—a stand of identical, interconnected trees.

2-2.description: A fortresslike tree, like a baobab or sequoia.

3-3.description: A living lock—a plant that seals evil below or blocks the path to a dungeon.

4-4.description: A lonely tree that stands atop a windswept mountain or amid a petrified forest.

5-5.description: A plant with magic fruit or remarkable seeds.

6-6.description: A shambling mound or treant that the dryad lives in or around as a Fey symbiote.

1-1.description: Colorful mineral formations.

2-2.description: Cooled magma in melted heaps.

3-3.description: Grass, moss, or plant roots.

4-4.description: Heaps of peat or decaying matter.

5-5.description: Mounds of sand studded with shells.

6-6.description: Rubble or pieces of a ruined structure.

7-7.description: Striking striations or bands of color.

8-8.description: Veins of iron or other ore.

1-1.description: Dramatic increase or decrease in temperature.

2-2.description: Gigantic coral reef or fungal forest.

3-3.description: Never-ending storm or whirlpool.

4-4.description: Passage to the Underdark or portal to an Elemental Plane.

5-5.description: Primeval or previously extinct animal population.

6-6.description: Rapidly growing rainforest.

7-7.description: River where previously there was none.

8-8.description: Series of dramatic rock formations.

1-1.description: Balanced, naturalistic, or suggestive of watching eyes.

2-2.description: Colorful, shadowy, or fluid or that vary depending on the viewer.

3-3.description: Comforting and gentle or that remind viewers of pleasant memories.

4-4.description: Disconnected parts, visible thoughts, or errant shapes.

5-5.description: Machinelike, stoic, symmetrical, or suggestive of judgment.

6-6.description: Morbid, menacing, or monstrous or that embody the viewer’s fears.

1-1.description: Amping up one another in preparation for a conflict or challenge.

2-2.description: Arguing over plans for battle, dinner, or how to spend the day.

3-3.description: Criticizing one another as they perform separate tasks.

4-4.description: Engaged in a staring contest.

5-5.description: Making polite small talk as if they were meeting for the first time.

6-6.description: Performatively ignoring one another.

7-7.description: Talking over an increasingly convoluted plot.

8-8.description: Trying to keep one another awake.

1-1.description: Colorful, superheated gases.

2-2.description: A column of diabolical or divine flame.

3-3.description: Crackling shapes that look like animals, fiends, skeletons, sprites, or other beings.

4-4.description: Flames that are predominantly white, blue, or a more unusual color.

5-5.description: The form of a calm or tormented humanoid.

6-6.description: Smoke that forms eerie shapes or symbols.

7-7.description: Soot that smells like cedar, cloves, incense, or burning meat.

8-8.description: Swirls of cinders and burning debris.

1-1.description: Arcane diagrams etched into it.

2-2.description: Flames like dramatic features, horns, or hair.

3-3.description: Fractured pieces that fly in unison.

4-4.description: An iron plate bolted over its mouth.

5-5.description: Lethal head trauma.

6-6.description: Mismatched animal teeth.

1-1.description: Animal parts among its humanlike pieces.

2-2.description: A disguise of makeup and heavy clothing.

3-3.description: Missing parts and exposed insides.

4-4.description: Parts serving unintended roles, like a body composed of dozens of hands.

5-5.description: Perfect features accented by beautiful stitching.

6-6.description: Visible mechanisms, bellows, and engines.

1-1.description: Cooking a meal of Underdark delicacies.

2-2.description: Performing a psychic song or “smell poem.”

3-3.description: Recovering and nursing fallen adventurers.

4-4.description: Revealing the location of helpful magic items.

5-5.description: Serving as a guide to a foe’s hidden lair.

6-6.description: Sharing excessive encouragement and praise.

Flumph Colors and Emotions.name: Flumph Colors and Emotions
Flumph Colors and Emotions.description: * Rarely seen; potentially no human equivalent

1-1.description: Color: Blue, Dark Emotion: Sadness

2-2.description: Color: Blue, Light Emotion: Happiness

3-3.description: Color: Green Emotion: Curiositiy

4-4.description: Color: Magenta Emotion: Unknown*

5-5.description: Color: Orange Emotion: Confusion

6-6.description: Color: Pink Emotion: Amusement

7-7.description: Color: Purple Emotion: Fear

8-8.description: Color: Red Emotion: Anger

1-1.description: Colorful, wandering pustules.

2-2.description: Excessive sweating of rainbow-hued fluids.

3-3.description: Patches of wriggling hair.

4-4.description: Veins that bulge and lurch under the skin.

2-2.description: Bee

1-1.description: Burls and bark on a giant tree.

2-2.description: Monuments in a graveyard or memorial.

3-3.description: Outcroppings on a cliff or rock formation

4-4.description: Reliefs on a sculpted gate or wall.

5-5.description: The petrified victims of a basilisk or medusa.

6-6.description: Rubble in a ruin or junkyard.

7-7.description: Statuary on a castle, mansion, or temple.

8-8.description: Stalactites or icicles on a cavern ceiling.

1-1.description: Cherubic with perpetually smiling features.

2-2.description: Crudely hewed or naturally formed.

3-3.description: Damaged or marred by mismatched pieces.

4-4.description: Dragon-like with polished stone scales.

5-5.description: Gothically fiendish with horns and a tail.

6-6.description: Useful, like an ornate podium or a pillar.

Gelatinous Cube Debris.name: Gelatinous Cube Debris
Gelatinous Cube Debris.description: Floating in the Gelatinous Cube Is A …

1-1.description: Chest or recently trapped mimic.

2-2.description: Collection of bubbles or rocks resembling eyes.

3-3.description: Key to a nearby door or coffer.

4-4.description: Remarkable weapon in need of repair.

5-5.description: Skeleton belonging to a famous adventurer.

6-6.description: Tablet bearing a mysterious message.

1-1.description: Another creature warped by dangerous magic.

2-2.description: The autonomous appendage of a chaotic deity, Far Realm entity, or star-spawn horror.

3-3.description: The experiment of an aberrant manipulator.

4-4.description: Part of the life cycle of some other Aberration.

5-5.description: A shape-shifter that lost control of its powers.

6-6.description: Someone cursed by a cult or vengeful deity.

1-1.description: Called on to deliver a message or mysterious parcel to or from Vlaakith the Lich Queen.

2-2.description: Encouraged by a disguised intellect devourer to seek out an elusive gith leader.

3-3.description: Entreated to aid githzerai fleeing the githyanki who destroyed their sanctuary.

4-4.description: Entrusted with renewing or disrupting the githyanki’s alliance with red dragons.

5-5.description: Invited to hunt illithids with githyanki.

6-6.description: Pressed to uncover a gith spy in a planar community or on a spelljamming ship.

7-7.description: Sent on a quest to discover the last known location of the hero Gith or Zerthimon.

8-8.description: Tasked with returning the blade of a fallen githyanki knight to the knight’s people.

1-1.description: Dedicates their impending victory to a deity, ruler, beloved noble, or member of the crowd.

2-2.description: Dresses in a monster-themed mask and cape.

3-3.description: Judges whether their foe fights honorably.

4-4.description: Leads the crowd in a rousing theme song.

5-5.description: Seeks to claim a trophy from a foe.

6-6.description: Takes advice from the crowd, omens, or a pet.

1-1.description: Cast a particular spell in the gorgon’s presence.

2-2.description: Keep their back to the gorgon or otherwise act like they don’t see the monster.

3-3.description: Offer it a drink of blood, water, or wine.

4-4.description: Recite a specific rhyme or sing a certain song.

5-5.description: Say its creator’s name backward.

6-6.description: Wear a mask, perhaps in the shape of a bull or an animal meaningful to the gorgon’s creator.

Green Hag Bargains.name: Green Hag Bargains
Green Hag Bargains.description: A Green Hag Trades Its Knowledge For …

1-1.description: A bargainer’s memories of a loved one.

2-2.description: The cauldron of a rival hag.

3-3.description: A favor to be redeemed when the hag wishes.

4-4.description: A flower from a hidden Feywild garden.

5-5.description: A gift given freely by a yugoloth.

6-6.description: A vial filled with a ruler’s tears.

1-1.description: Advanced viruses, each the clone of all other grell. They exist only to feed and spread.

2-2.description: The larvae of another creature and require electrically charged environs to reproduce.

3-3.description: Seeking to escape some catastrophe or terror lurking in the depths.

4-4.description: Supernaturally connected to ravenous alien beings and serve as their feeding appendages.

5-5.description: Vestiges of an ancient evil that will return if grells collectively consume enough creatures.

6-6.description: Without souls, but convinced they can attain souls by eating certain beings.

1-1.description: Attack anything in the skies near their lairs.

2-2.description: Curse their killers. Those who slay a griffon face the enmity of all animals.

3-3.description: Lay eggs with remarkable healing properties.

4-4.description: Prefer the taste of horses over all other prey.

5-5.description: Serve the first creature they see after hatching.

6-6.description: Won’t attack those with royal blood.

1-1.description: Carving caves to serve as illithid outposts.

2-2.description: Hiding the threat of mind flayers beneath fake, purposefully crude dwellings.

3-3.description: Pretending to be helpful and luring travelers into false senses of security.

4-4.description: Raiding surface communities and tempting other creatures to pursue it into illithid traps.

1-1.description: The last words of an ancient sage or leader.

2-2.description: The location of a hidden city or continent.

3-3.description: A magic word, password, or riddle’s answer.

4-4.description: The names of all who have told it stories.

5-5.description: An otherwise forgotten ritual or spell.

6-6.description: Recipes using regional ingredients.

7-7.description: Stories of forgotten gods and local spirits.

8-8.description: The vulnerabilities of a legendary monster.

1-1.description: Carry its master’s palanquin through the air.

2-2.description: Defend a remarkable treasure or piece of armor by incorporating the item into its being.

3-3.description: Imitate a dead or imprisoned hero by using their armor and weapons.

4-4.description: Perform as a laborer or servant.

5-5.description: Serve in a legion formed from the armors of a land’s ancient defenders.

6-6.description: Stand sentry in a gallery of mundane armors.

1-1.description: Low-hanging moon, star, or other solar body.

2-2.description: Magical garden on a floating island.

3-3.description: Mountaintop with a view of a giant geoglyph.

4-4.description: Nest full of hippogriff eggs atop a spire.

5-5.description: Portal to the Feywild or an Upper Plane.

6-6.description: Lost ruin hidden by clouds or fog.

1-1.description: Build a vessel to carry hobgoblin armies to new conquests.

2-2.description: Capture monsters and train them to fight.

3-3.description: Collapse a region into the Underdark so riches can be sifted from the ruins.

4-4.description: Construct a giant machine to strip resources.

5-5.description: Convince devils, dragons, or hobgoblins from Acheron to invade an enemy land.

6-6.description: Help shortsighted merchants undermine a government or despoil the environment.

1-1.description: Bat-like with tattered wings.

2-2.description: Made of soft metal and delicate gears.

3-3.description: Marked with its creator’s symbol.

4-4.description: Similar to those of a winged humanoid.

5-5.description: Sprouting flowers and leaves.

6-6.description: Suggestive of its creator’s appearance.

7-7.description: Underdeveloped and fleshy with beady eyes.

8-8.description: Woven and patchwork, like a well-loved toy.

4-4.description: Kot

Hydra Lairs.name: Hydra Lairs
Hydra Lairs.description: The Hydra Lurks Where It Does To …

1-1.description: Ensure none claim the weapon of a fallen hero.

2-2.description: Defend the home of a wise but sinister oracle.

3-3.description: Guard a magical herb that blooms once a year.

4-4.description: Protect a font of poison that pollutes a river.

1-1.description: An angry family member or authority figure.

2-2.description: Being chased through the wilderness.

3-3.description: Being devoured by animals or monsters.

4-4.description: Falling, drowning, or suffocating.

5-5.description: A ruinous public embarrassment.

6-6.description: A shadowy intruder or monstrous silhouette.

7-7.description: A traumatic past event.

8-8.description: A visitor with an eerie or enigmatic message.

1-1.description: Block a door that has never been opened, moving only when a prophecy is fulfilled.

2-2.description: Exhale poison gas whenever it can, pausing only when someone speaks a passphrase.

3-3.description: Pose as a statue until a community’s hour of greatest need.

4-4.description: Stand atop the resting place of a powerful magic item.

1-1.description: Guiding them into wildernesses, then abandoning them.

2-2.description: Mapping a shortcut through a monster’s lair.

3-3.description: Putting them to sleep, then stealing mounts or supplies.

4-4.description: Sharing the location of hidden treasure, which turns out to be sunlight on sand or water.

1-1.description: Abducts the vessel of a leader or another important community member.

2-2.description: Attacks a community from below using flooded ruins, hidden aquifers, or sewers.

3-3.description: Breaks a lighthouse or seaside tower, carrying it and the occupants to a secret island.

4-4.description: Calls down lightning on any ship that enters its aquatic territory.

5-5.description: Carries ships to an inescapable sargassum.

6-6.description: Dams a river or cuts off a city’s sea access.

7-7.description: Devours all sea life near a fishing community, threatening it with ruin.

8-8.description: Masterminds an invasion from the sea by merfolk, sahuagin, or storm giants.

1-1.description: An arena scattered with weapons made from crustacean shells.

2-2.description: A gallery of hibernating chuuls.

3-3.description: A garden of mussels and tide pool creatures that whisper secrets.

4-4.description: A hidden shrine with a patchwork depiction of a new kuo-toa deity.

5-5.description: The lavish chamber of an animal or monster said to be prophetic, lucky, or literate.

6-6.description: A pool filled with jellyfish, eels, or fish roe that glow in organized patterns.

7-7.description: A punishment chamber exposed to the light of the surface.

1-1.description: Bring it a possession from a ruler or noble.

2-2.description: Create a map of a dungeon or ruin.

3-3.description: Escort it through a nearby community’s gate.

4-4.description: Place a strange idol in a specific site or home.

5-5.description: Remove a magic item’s curse, then return it.

6-6.description: Slay a monster and retrieve a specific organ.

1-1.description: A bottle or puzzle box inscribed with sigils.

2-2.description: A contract folded into a paper figure.

3-3.description: The first magic item the lich created.

4-4.description: A hollow figurine of a deity or monster.

5-5.description: An hourglass with its sands floating in stasis.

6-6.description: A locket or signet ring with a noble crest.

7-7.description: A rune-etched egg.

8-8.description: The skull of the lich’s mentor.

1-1.description: An astronomer who draws magic from stars.

2-2.description: An author who writes about the occult.

3-3.description: A magical engineer who creates wonders.

4-4.description: An oracle who interprets omens.

5-5.description: A prodigy with a remarkable magical heritage.

6-6.description: A psion whose powers manifest as spells.

7-7.description: A scholar investigating ancient lore.

8-8.description: A soothsayer who advises rulers.

9-9.description: A war mage who aids soldiers in battle.

10-10.description: A witch who shares secret wisdom.

1-1.description: Attack a particular foe.

2-2.description: Create a distraction.

3-3.description: Give up a captive or corpse.

4-4.description: Let a group navigate its territory unharmed.

5-5.description: Let someone pretend to slay it in battle.

6-6.description: Scare or threaten someone.

7-7.description: Serve a creature as a steed until the sun sets.

8-8.description: Try to locate something from its vantage point in the sky.

1-1.description: Born a medusa and lives unaware of whatever curse or circumstances afflicted its ancestor.

2-2.description: Created by a god and tasked with guarding a treasure or secret.

3-3.description: A cultist who made a fiendish bargain and enjoyed rewards that have since faded.

4-4.description: An explorer transformed and compelled to defend a cursed ruin.

5-5.description: A vain noble whose magical attempt to gain eternal beauty backfired.

6-6.description: The victim of a bite from a magical serpent or reptilian god in disguise.

1-1.description: Access to a planar portal.

2-2.description: Information valued by its true master.

3-3.description: A lair where it can bring others of its kind.

4-4.description: Magic weapons or armor.

5-5.description: The right to loot holy sites in places it conquers.

6-6.description: Souls, whether as larvae or captured spirits.

1-1.description: Blot out the sun so their Underdark-dwelling servants can invade the surface.

2-2.description: Create a new monstrous fusion between mind flayers and a legendary monster.

3-3.description: Forge a psionic network uniting illithid colonies.

4-4.description: Replace world leaders with intellect devourers.

5-5.description: Restore a vessel to travel through Wildspace.

6-6.description: Sacrifice the mental energy of a planet’s populace to take control of a githyanki bastion.

1-1.description: A multilevel mine or sewer.

2-2.description: Multiple mazes connected by magic portals.

3-3.description: A poisonous swamp with labyrinthine paths.

4-4.description: The ruins of a buried palace or temple.

1-1.description: Create a clockwork outpost to monitor the balance of obscure planar forces.

2-2.description: Ensure neither side gains the upper hand in a conflict between good and evil.

3-3.description: Excavate a portal to another plane.

4-4.description: Find a lost contingent of modrons.

5-5.description: Reactivate a titanic but lost modron device.

6-6.description: Remove a forest, mountain, or city before the arrival of a modron procession.

7-7.description: Seal off a planar rift or wild magic zone.

8-8.description: Wage war with demons, slaadi, or chaotic Fey.

1-1.description: Open a portal to the past, when its power was at its height.

2-2.description: Perform a ritual that can be attempted only once every eight hundred years.

3-3.description: Reclaim and resurrect a loved one’s corpse.

4-4.description: Reconquer the lands that once composed its empire.

5-5.description: Recover the pieces of its lost heart.

6-6.description: Replace its descendant as the ruler of a realm.

7-7.description: Sacrifice a thousand souls to its god in return for true life.

8-8.description: Transform the people of an entire nation into Undead servants.

1-1.description: Defend a holy site it was created to protect.

2-2.description: Obey the summons of a mummy lord.

3-3.description: Oppose an enemy who has returned to life.

4-4.description: Protect its descendants from an ancient threat.

5-5.description: Punish the progeny of those who cursed it.

6-6.description: Reclaim treasures robbed from its crypt.

7-7.description: Serve whoever speaks the prayer on its tomb.

8-8.description: Slay anyone who sets eyes on it.

1-1.description: The champion or messenger of an evil deity.

2-2.description: A group of joyriding imps or quasits.

3-3.description: An innocent soul trapped on the wild Fiend.

4-4.description: A lore-hunting mage, cultist, or lich.

5-5.description: A night hag herding larvae between planes.

6-6.description: A wicked cavalier, such as a death knight, an erinyes, an incubus, or a vampire.

Ochre Jelly Leftovers.name: Ochre Jelly Leftovers
Ochre Jelly Leftovers.description: After a Meal, the Ochre Jelly Leaves Behind …

1-1.description: A bone etched with a word or an eerie symbol.

2-2.description: Broken dragonborn or tiefling horns.

3-3.description: An ornate prosthetic limb.

4-4.description: The skeleton of an explorer’s pet (perhaps a small dog, monkey, or parrot).

5-5.description: A skull with gold teeth worth 1d4 GP.

6-6.description: A spotless suit of metal armor.

1-1.description: Charming people to perform nasty tricks.

2-2.description: Claiming a bridge, gate, shrine, or trail and trying to eat anyone who comes near.

3-3.description: Luring other monsters to the settlement.

4-4.description: Playing drums that keep everyone awake.

1-1.description: Disguises its tentacles with garbage puppets.

2-2.description: Sings an enticing song in Otyugh.

3-3.description: Telepathically transmits a message like “Happy good stuff here!” or “Help now! I’m too delicious?”

4-4.description: Telepathically transmits an image of a large gemstone, crooked weapon, or soggy pastry.

1-1.description: Evidence of previous occupants, like bandits, wolves, or dragons.

2-2.description: Heaps of regurgitated pellets studded with coins or other treasure.

3-3.description: A nest with 1d6 owlbear eggs.

4-4.description: Passages through the earth or hollow trees.

1-1.description: Bearing the gear of a hero the pegasus aided.

2-2.description: Offering magical fruit or holy spring water.

3-3.description: Singing a song in Celestial, Druidic, or Sylvan.

4-4.description: Wearing the garb of an ancient heroic order.

1-1.description: The hearts grant an evil wish.

2-2.description: It reverts to its original form.

3-3.description: A new peryton hatches from each heart.

4-4.description: A portal opens to the Lower Planes.

Petrified Basilisk Victims.name: Petrified Basilisk Victims
Petrified Basilisk Victims.description: A Basilisk Used Its Gaze to Petrify …

1-1.description: An adventurer with an ornate key hanging around their neck.

2-2.description: Animals like bats, bears, deer, or goats.

3-3.description: A climber clinging to a stalactite.

4-4.description: Itself using a large mirror or shiny surface.

5-5.description: A mimic disguised as a chest full of treasure.

6-6.description: A monster such as an umber hulk or a troglodyte.

7-7.description: Someone caught in a comic pose or making a regrettable face.

8-8.description: A victim now being used as a nest for insects or other vermin.

1-1.description: A captain’s hat

2-2.description: Crossbones

3-3.description: Crossed blades

4-4.description: An eye patch

5-5.description: Lightning bolts

6-6.description: A mug of ale

7-7.description: A tattoo

8-8.description: A treasure chest

2-2.description: Smok

3-3.description: Czart

4-4.description: Koza

5-5.description: Kraken

6-6.description: Morzan

7-7.description: Skull

8-8.description: Whale

Planetar Quests.name: Planetar Quests
Planetar Quests.description: The Planetar Entreats a Mortal Hero To …

1-1.description: Convince a villain to meet with the angel.

2-2.description: Find a loved one a villain believes is dead.

3-3.description: Heal the loved one of an evil ruler.

4-4.description: Inspire the defenders of a besieged holy site.

5-5.description: Recover and destroy an evil Artifact.

6-6.description: Reveal the true name of a devil to banish it.

1-1.description: Keeps returning a discarded item.

2-2.description: Leaves footprints on vertical surfaces.

3-3.description: Makes noises like someone trapped in a wall.

4-4.description: Organizes a pack’s contents across the floor.

5-5.description: Playfully puppets a corpse or doll.

6-6.description: Removes bedding while someone sleeps.

7-7.description: Sticks knives or weapons in the ceiling.

8-8.description: Uncannily stacks books, furniture, or utensils.

1-1.description: An ascetic who keeps wicked spirits at bay.

2-2.description: An elder who speaks for the dead.

3-3.description: An exorcist who hunts wicked spirits.

4-4.description: A follower of a god no one has heard of.

5-5.description: A mediator and teacher of traditional ways.

6-6.description: A philosopher devoted to a concept, multiversal view, or plane of existence.

7-7.description: The reincarnation of an ancient faith leader.

8-8.description: A ritualist who uses tinctures and performances to access the divine.

9-9.description: A shaman whose medicines ease many ills.

10-10.description: A zealot who wages war for a divine cause.

1-1.description: Flamboyant accessories it can wear.

2-2.description: Mementos from a lost friend or master.

3-3.description: Outlandish delicacies—like axe beak-egg omelets or mammoth-milk cheese.

4-4.description: The possessions of a sibling, rival, or master.

5-5.description: Shiny gifts, from gems to abalone shells.

6-6.description: Soft bedding and stuffed toys.

7-7.description: A specific cook’s signature dessert.

8-8.description: Time-consuming beauty treatments.

9-9.description: To hear a bedtime story or favorite song.

1-1.description: The burial litter of a lost hero.

2-2.description: A caravan wagon full of trade goods.

3-3.description: A live elephant.

4-4.description: [[/r 1d4#Eggs]]{1d4 eggs} larger than adult humans

5-5.description: Someone marooned in the nest.

6-6.description: A statue of a knight riding a rearing steed.

1-1.description: Areas that trigger traps.

2-2.description: Caverns filled with smoke or gas.

3-3.description: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.xgLA30rErlkjIPAK#dead-magic-zone]{Dead magic}* or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.xgLA30rErlkjIPAK#wild-magic-zone]{wild magic}* zones.

4-4.description: The lair of a creature it is trying to bait out.

5-5.description: A nest of rats, insects, or other vermin.

6-6.description: Patches of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.lVkdQivhFHqVmxg7#brown-mold]{brown mold}* or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.lVkdQivhFHqVmxg7#green-slime]{green slime}*.

7-7.description: Pools of magma or boiling water.

8-8.description: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.lVkdQivhFHqVmxg7#razorvine]{Razorvine}* or similar dangerous plants.

1-1.description: Coyote

2-2.description: Crow

3-3.description: Słoń

4-4.description: Jaszczurka

5-5.description: Mouse

6-6.description: Sowa

7-7.description: Łosoś

8-8.description: Pająk

1-1.description: Animal furs, bones, horns, and claws.

2-2.description: Beehives or wasp nests over a wicker frame.

3-3.description: A carved pumpkin atop a body of thick vines.

4-4.description: Nets, flotsam, grapnels, and fishing tackle.

5-5.description: Oversize stuffed animal or mannequin parts.

6-6.description: Rusty armor and torture devices.

7-7.description: A sackcloth head atop straw-stuffed clothes.

8-8.description: Wedding clothes that were never worn.

Sea Hag Disguises.name: Sea Hag Disguises
Sea Hag Disguises.description: The Sea Hag Takes the Form of A …

1-1.description: Captive and claims nearby villagers bound them and left them to drown.

2-2.description: Castaway and shares a cursed item’s location with would-be rescuers.

3-3.description: Healer and passes off poisons as medicine.

4-4.description: Panic-spreading prophesier of doom.

5-5.description: Ship captain and delivers passengers to the hag’s pet sea monster.

6-6.description: Wounded sailor and claims their ship was destroyed by merfolk or other peaceful people.

1-1.description: A distorted stalker that lurks in the woods.

2-2.description: A fiend that dwells near a wicked ritual site.

3-3.description: Grasping hands that haunt a miser’s home.

4-4.description: A grim storybook character that follows those who speak its name.

5-5.description: Its target, acting in eerie pantomime.

6-6.description: An ominous priest that haunts a defiled site.

1-1.description: Covered in vibrant alien or Feywild blooms.

2-2.description: Hauling a rune-etched menhir in its torso.

3-3.description: Infested with vermin or fungi.

4-4.description: Made up of knotty vines entangling skeletons.

5-5.description: Mutated and leaking glowing pollution.

6-6.description: The remains of an ancient tree or a treant.

Skeleton Pantomimes.name: Skeleton Pantomimes
Skeleton Pantomimes.description: Left to Its Own Devices, the Skeleton …

1-1.description: Delivers meal salvers or ages-old correspondence to the crypt of its dead master.

2-2.description: Endlessly trains in battle with other skeletons, despite being hacked to animate splinters.

3-3.description: Mimics ways it entertained itself in life, such as acting, dancing, or reading.

4-4.description: Performs a familiar task, such as cleaning, cooking, mining, or praying.

5-5.description: Repeats its final moments of life.

6-6.description: Stands guard at the post it protected in life.

1-1.description: A community afflicted by curses, grudges, plagues, or tragedies.

2-2.description: An evil Artifact or a deadly magical device.

3-3.description: The place where a villain died or is buried.

4-4.description: The lair of a Fiend or an Undead.

5-5.description: A portal to the Lower Planes, Negative Plane, or Shadowfell.

6-6.description: The sanctuary of a necromancer or death cult.

7-7.description: A secluded monument binding wicked souls.

8-8.description: The site of a disaster or mass death.

1-1.description: Artifacts, their locations, and their owners.

2-2.description: Betrayals by infernal allies or other devils.

3-3.description: Crimes or deceptions by influential leaders.

4-4.description: Prophecies or secrets hidden by gods.

5-5.description: The identities of incognito individuals.

6-6.description: The movements of extraplanar armies.

1-1.description: A character is to blame for its recent failures.

2-2.description: It has been evicted from its rightful home.

3-3.description: Locals have reneged on an age-old bargain.

4-4.description: Other creatures are mocking it.

5-5.description: A rival is spying on it.

6-6.description: Someone’s treasure rightfully belongs to it.

1-1.description: A bard or traveling performer.

2-2.description: A captive or servant of a monster or villain.

3-3.description: A dignitary or traveler from a distant land.

4-4.description: A visitor from a different time or world.

1-1.description: The attic or furniture of a ruined building.

2-2.description: A cave or narrow crevice.

3-3.description: A hollow tree or thicket.

4-4.description: The remains of a gigantic, dead creature.

1-1.description: Allow only those wearing ritual garb to pass.

2-2.description: Cast Slow on and aid in apprehending anyone who touches a city’s prized relic.

3-3.description: Destroy a dam or bridge at the command of one bearing a ruler’s medallion of office.

4-4.description: Obey whoever places a missing crest in its chest, then deactivate for a year.

5-5.description: Reveal a hidden passage to those who recite a leader’s final words.

6-6.description: Watch for and do battle with the type of monster that slew the hero it resembles.

1-1.description: Adopting the form of a lost loved one.

2-2.description: Charming someone close to its target.

3-3.description: Isolating them from their loved ones.

4-4.description: Manipulating events to bring surprise fortune.

5-5.description: Posing as a flattering underling.

6-6.description: Taking the form of one in need of protection.

1-1.description: Evidence of a magic spell reflected back on its caster, like Ice Knife or Melf’s Acid Arrow .

2-2.description: Massive footprints or claw marks.

3-3.description: A shattered mountain or diverted river.

4-4.description: A russet scale the size of a knight’s shield.

1-1.description: Blessed by a god and grows magic fruit.

2-2.description: Growing atop the entrance to a dungeon or portal to the Feywild.

3-3.description: Home to a community of pixies or sprites.

4-4.description: The last lore keeper of lost druidic knowledge.

5-5.description: Rooted on a hero’s burial mound and animates trees that look like questing knights.

6-6.description: Scarred by a fire and holds the bones of the arsonist who started it in a hollow.

1-1.description: Convince cultists their god has forsaken them.

2-2.description: Destabilize a nation and rule the chaos.

3-3.description: Incite a calamity and hold a world hostage.

4-4.description: Provoke hostilities between immortal armies and sell magic weapons to both sides.

5-5.description: Steal an invention and slay all who know of it.

6-6.description: Unleash fiendish hordes on a foe’s homeland.

1-1.description: The breath of an infamous god or monster.

2-2.description: A guardian of a hidden portal or magical site.

3-3.description: The lingering violent thoughts of someone killed in a great battle.

4-4.description: A manifestation of uncontrolled magic.

5-5.description: A servant of an evil elemental ruler such as Yan-C-Bin (the Elemental Prince of Evil Air).

6-6.description: Unable to complete its duty and tries to create circumstances allowing it to fulfill its task.

1-1.description: Among the roots of a dead tree.

2-2.description: At the bottom of a stagnant pool.

3-3.description: A coffin filled with grave dirt.

4-4.description: A large pot full of blood or vinegar.

5-5.description: A space accessible only by shape-shifting.

6-6.description: Within a statue or suit of armor.

1-1.description: A bodyguard who protects a noble.

2-2.description: A cavalry officer with an unusual steed.

3-3.description: A crusader who fights for a divine cause.

4-4.description: A duelist who claims to be unbeatable.

5-5.description: A gate guard who asks nonsensical questions.

6-6.description: A grizzled veteran who trains new recruits.

7-7.description: A hunter skilled at slaying specific monsters.

8-8.description: A retired general who is weary of battle.

9-9.description: A volunteer with a homemade weapon.

10-10.description: A young mercenary trying to prove their skill.

1-1.description: Chilling or near-boiling temperatures.

2-2.description: Energetic effervescence.

3-3.description: Muddy, polluted, or crystal-clear water.

4-4.description: Seaweed, tiny fish, or other sea life.

1-1.description: Legendary weapon or symbol of rulership.

2-2.description: Proving ground for prophesied heroes.

3-3.description: Rare species of magical plant or animal.

4-4.description: Sacred fountain with magical waters.

Wight Motives.name: Wight Motives
Wight Motives.description: The Wight Returned from the Dead To …

1-1.description: Challenge anyone who passes near its grave on a certain cursed night.

2-2.description: Conquer the land it believes it should rule.

3-3.description: Continue the crimes it was executed for.

4-4.description: Follow the foul master it served in life.

5-5.description: Honor an oath it left unfulfilled in life.

6-6.description: Obey the cult or deity that gave it unlife.

7-7.description: Prove it was the greatest warrior to ever live.

8-8.description: Seek its stolen heart or other treasure.

1-1.description: An abandoned structure ready to collapse.

2-2.description: An ambush by hungry ghouls or vampires.

3-3.description: A dreaded ruin that curses those who enter.

4-4.description: The lair of a predator, like a bear or wyvern.

5-5.description: Patches of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.lVkdQivhFHqVmxg7#brown-mold]{brown mold}* or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.lVkdQivhFHqVmxg7#green-slime]{green slime.}*

6-6.description: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.lVkdQivhFHqVmxg7#quicksand-pit]{Quicksand}* or pools covered in @UUID[Compendium.dnd-dungeon-masters-guide.content.JournalEntry.dmgDmsToolbox000.JournalEntryPage.xgLA30rErlkjIPAK#thin-ice]{thin ice.}*

1-1.description: The blasphemous magic of a cursed location.

2-2.description: The exorcised evil of a redeemed villain.

3-3.description: A legendary villain who returns once a century.

4-4.description: Locals’ fear of a superstition or legend.

5-5.description: The memory of a tragedy.

6-6.description: A profane idea or foul piece of lore.

7-7.description: The torment of one or more suffering souls.

8-8.description: The viciousness of a profane Artifact.

9-9.description: The vile dreams of a slumbering god.

10-10.description: The voracity of a life-hungry realm, such as the Shadowfell or Negative Plane.

1-1.description: Adamantine or mithral.

2-2.description: Coins minted by a long-dead empire.

3-3.description: Fossils or petrified wood.

4-4.description: A gem that’s part of a magic item.

5-5.description: The keystone of a great arch or bridge.

6-6.description: Parts of a galeb duhr or stone golem.

7-7.description: A piece of a meteor or moon.

8-8.description: The stone crowning a mountain peak.

Yuan-ti Transformations.name: Yuan-ti Transformations
Yuan-ti Transformations.description: A Yuan-ti Gained Its Snake Features From …

1-1.description: Bargaining parts of its soul to a pantheon of serpentine demigods.

2-2.description: A curse laid on its people in the distant past.

3-3.description: The dream-venom of Merrshaulk, a slumbering snake god.

4-4.description: Experiments by spirit nagas or other yuan-ti.

5-5.description: A ritual involving the skin of a fiendish snake.

6-6.description: Trials to excise its “weak” human parts.

1-1.description: Claim a portal with strategic importance.

2-2.description: Enlist monsters as allies or beasts of war.

3-3.description: Destroy a city harboring enemy cultists.

4-4.description: Liberate an imprisoned fiendish ally.
